questions.question: What is good practice before transmitting on a frequency?

options.text: Listen first to check the channel is clear

questions.reason: Listening first reduces interference and helps ensure respectful spectrum sharing.

questions.question: How should you generally choose transmit power for a contact?

options.text: Use the minimum power needed for reliable communication
Match nearby stations regardless of need

questions.reason: Using minimum necessary power reduces unnecessary interference and is good operating practice.

questions.question: In an emergency on-air, what should be your priority?

options.text: Keep transmissions brief, clear, and relevant

questions.reason: Emergency traffic should be concise and relevant so essential information is passed quickly and clearly.

questions.question: Why is it helpful to use the phonetic alphabet when giving a callsign?
questions.reason: Phonetics make letters easier to copy accurately, especially in noisy or weak-signal conditions.

questions.question: What is good practice when using a repeater?

options.text: Use the highest power regardless of need

questions.reason: Short pauses let others join in and allow the repeater to reset properly between transmissions.

questions.question: If you find a frequency already in use, what should you normally do?

options.text: Move to another clear frequency or wait

questions.reason: Good operating practice means avoiding interference and sharing frequencies courteously.

questions.question: What is the purpose of calling CQ?

options.text: To invite any station to make contact

questions.reason: CQ is a general call inviting other stations to respond.

questions.question: Why should you avoid very long overs during a net or repeater QSO?

options.text: To allow breaks and participation by others

questions.reason: Shorter overs and pauses improve flow and allow others to join or pass priority traffic.

questions.question: What is a good response if another operator reports your audio is distorted?

options.text: Check microphone gain and station settings

questions.reason: Checking settings after a credible report is good operating practice and reduces interference risk.

questions.question: What is generally best before changing to another frequency with a contact?

options.text: Agree the new frequency clearly with the other station

questions.reason: Clear coordination avoids confusion and keeps operation efficient.

questions.question: When passing message content on-air, what style is usually best?

options.text: Clear, concise wording with key details repeated if needed

questions.reason: Plain, concise communication improves accuracy, especially under weak-signal conditions.

questions.question: What is appropriate if priority or emergency traffic appears on your frequency?

options.text: Stand by and keep the frequency clear

questions.reason: Routine traffic should cease so priority communications can pass quickly and safely.
